title: Peponidium celastroides
taxonomy: Peponidium celastroides is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Peponidium, which is a group of evergreen shrubs or small trees native to Madagascar. The Rubiaceae family is a large and diverse family of flowering plants, commonly known as the madder or coffee family. The classification of Peponidium celastroides is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape and flower structure. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and taxonomic position of this plant species within the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Peponidium celastroides, also known as the Madagascar St. John's Wort, is a plant species native to the island of Madagascar, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and fever, due to its potential health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
